title: Tarkin (superweapon)
content:

Research/Legends|Imperial Department of Military Research]]''[[Death Star Owner's Technical Manual]]'' |line= |model=''Tarkin'' [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]][[Star Wars (1977) 51|''Star |armament=*[[Superlaser/Legends|Ionic cannon superlaser]] (1){{CSWECite|III|220|Tarkin}} *[[Tractor beam/Legends|Tractor beam]][[Star Wars (1977) 52|''Star Wars'' (1977) 52]] |complement=*[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] *[[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|Darth Vader's TIE Advanced x1]] |bays= |escapepods= |crew=*35,705 (operational staff) *8,158 (gunners)''[[The Essential Guide to Characters]]'' |skeleton= |passengers=*105,417: **[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables=Estimated 2 years |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |region= |sector= |system=[[Patriim system/Legends|Patriim system]] |coordinates= |xyz= |orbited=[[Hockaleg]] |distance= |routes= |role=Weapons testbed platform{{DB|location|deathstarii|Death Star II}}{{DB|technology|superlaser|superlaser}} |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |constructed= |destroyed=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] |retired= |battles=*[[Destruction of Aeten II]]{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}} *[[Infiltration of the Tarkin|Infiltration of the ''Tarkin'']] |owners= |namedcrew=*[[Berl (stormtrooper)|Berl]] *[[Brunok]] {{C|Construction crew}} *[[Unidentified Tarkin construction crewman|Unidentified construction crewman]] {{C|Construction crew}} |captains=*[[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] *[[General/Legends|General]] [[Biel]] *[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Nord (Imperial)|Nord]] *[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Voal]] *[[Unidentified Imperial admiral (Tarkin)|Tarkin's admiral]] |registry= |aliases= |population= |interest= |services= |sectors= |modules= }} {{Quote|…why hasn't the Empire constructed a second battle station like the ''Death Star'' that almost destroyed our base on Yavin? Now we ''know'' why: they've been secretly channeling resources into the construction of a weapon that could be far ''worse!''|General Carlist Rieekan|}} The '''''Tarkin''''', or the '''Tarkin Project''', was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] modeled in principle after the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] and named in honor of the late [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]]. The ''Tarkin'' combined the first [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] offensive capabilities in the form of a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]-destroying [[Superlaser/Legends|ionic cannon superlaser]] with a [[Deflector shield/Legends|defensive shield generator]] and a set of [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. Imperial [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Bevel Lemelisk]] designed the ''Tarkin'', at the behest of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] following the Death Star's destruction at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], as a prototype weapons testbed for the construction of the [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class Super Star Destroyer]], a massive [[Warship/Legends|warship]] armed with a [[Starship/Legends|ship]]-mounted [[Axial superlaser/Legends|superlaser]]. With technological refinements, the ''Tarkin'' improved upon the mistakes learned from the first Death Star, including the absence of the single design flaw—an exposed [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]]—that proved to be the original battlestation's undoing, in the name of the development of a [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], built concurrently with the ''Tarkin''. The Empire secretly began constructing the ''Tarkin'' in the [[Patriim system/Legends|Patriim system]], above the [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] planet [[Hockaleg]], under the supervision of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Nod Warfield]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Martio Batch]] requisitioned the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} destructive superlaser to [[Destruction of Aeten II|smash]] the planet [[Aeten II/Legends|Aeten II]]—the only ever offensive application of the ''Tarkin'' superweapon—releasing millions of the planet's deeply buried [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], which Batch gathered to recreate the stygium screen [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]] for his [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] development project. Once learning of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} existence shortly thereafter, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] dispatched an elite commando team consisting of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—the only Rebels to have been aboard the first Death Star and survived—to the Patriim [[Star system/Legends|system]] to [[Infiltration of the Tarkin|infiltrate the ''Tarkin'']] and destroy the superweapon from within before it could be completed. Despite the presence of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and a traitorous group of the superweapon's [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officers]], who sought to both kill Vader and stop the Rebel [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]], Organa drew upon a recollection of the original Death Star's [[Death Star plans/Legends|technical schematics]] to rewire the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser firing mechanism to fire on itself. The ''Tarkin'' later self-destructed upon activation of the sabotaged superlaser in a failed attempt to obliterate the Rebels as they fled from the battlestation. ==Description== {{Quote|Worse than a ''Death Star?''"
"I'm afraid so, Commander. They've taken the Death Star's main offensive battery, the ionic cannon—and have built around it a set of engines and defensive shield generators unmatched by anything we've ever encountered! This weapon has been christened "the Tarkin" and should it ever become operational—we may as well place our own hands in the Empire's shackles!|Commander Luke Skywalker and General Carlist Rieekan|}} [[File:OooPrettyColors-MSW52.jpg|thumb|left|180px|The ''Tarkin''{{'s}} ionic cannon, primed for destruction]] The ''Tarkin'' [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]], also known as the Tarkin Project,''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen, Revised]]'' was a giant [[Planet/Legends|planetary]]-scale [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] following the same basic principles of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]. The ''Tarkin'' combined the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] primary offensive battery and massive [[Superlaser/Legends|ionic cannon superlaser]], capable of shattering a planet in the same mold as the Death Star's, with a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|defensive shield generator]] system and a set of [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. The ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser, comprising a central concave dish encircled by a series of secondary tributary nodes, dominated the battlestation's external forward superstructure. The awesome size of the ''Tarkin'' reduced the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] normally-fearsome [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] to diminutives by comparison. Unlike its predecessor, the ''Tarkin'' was constructed without the single design flaw—an exposed [[Thermal exhaust port/Legends|thermal exhaust port]]—that led to the Death Star's destruction. Set back from the forward superlaser dish was the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} central command tower and [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]]. The command tower resembled the same trapezoidal design found on [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], sans the twin [[ISD-72x deflector shield generator dome|deflector shield generator domes]]. The bridge, too, resembled the layout of the ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer in a general sense, with a wide angular [[Viewport/Legends|viewport]] and a command walkway. A series of double-tiered seated command stations were circularly-arrayed before the bridge's viewports. The battlestation's interior was divided into sections and levels identified by letters and numerals—K-level and sections TH-10 and TH-11, for example. The interior also housed the installation's main power [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]], the primary external [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam generator]], the [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] bay, [[Hangar/Legends|hangar bays]] hosting the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} [[Starship/Legends|starship]] complement, and the superweapon's complex activating mechanism. A series of [[Turbolift/Legends|service lifts]] offered rapid vertical transport between the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} many decks. A [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] guard force patrolled the ''Tarkin'' and provided security for the battlestation's sensitive areas, restricting access only to those with proper clearance, and a series of [[Gunner/Legends|gunners]] controlled the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser firing mechanism. The ''Tarkin'' also maintained a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] contingent for close-range aerial support. ==History== {{Quote|So that's it. The problem is simple—the ''solution'' isn't! Since the ''Tarkin'' was built without the single flaw that allowed us to stop the Death Star, a direct attack would be suicide! Our only chance for success is to get a small group of commandos ''inside'' the ''Tarkin''—and destroy it from within!|General Carlist Rieekan|}} ===Design origins and destruction of Aeten II=== [[File:Tarkin_front_and_side.png|thumb|left|200px|Front and sideviews of the ''Tarkin''.]] The ''Tarkin'' was a direct descendant, in both form and function, of the Empire's first Death Star, destroyed in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. Incensed at the loss of his weapon of doom, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] ordered [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]] [[Bevel Lemelisk]], the Death Star's original designer, to produce a resurrected Death Star, even larger and more powerful than the first.''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]'' At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] likewise had already ordered the construction of a pair of massive [[Warship/Legends|warships]] equipped with [[Axial superlaser/Legends|axial superlasers]]—the [[Eclipse-class dreadnought|''Eclipse''-class dreadnought]]. To keep the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] from discovering the new Death Star and to test the ship-mounted superlaser of his new warships, the Emperor tasked Lemelisk with developing a prototype of the ''Eclipse''-class into an operational weapons platform.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' [[File:The_Tarkin_by_Stephan_Martiniere.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Tarkin'' takes shape in the Patriim system.]] Lemelisk first set to designing a superlaser weapons testbed, similar in principle to the first [[Death Star prototype]] constructed in the [[Maw Installation]], that corrected the mistakes learned from the first Death Star's design with technological refinements. [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] eventually christened this weapons testbed, essentially a scaled-down version of the Death Star, the "''Tarkin''", in memory of the late [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], the Death Star's former [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]].''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' After Lemelisk's design team completed their work on the ''Tarkin'', they began designing the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star's successor]]. Meanwhile, construction on the superlaser testbed began at the [[Patriim shipyards]] in the [[Patriim system/Legends|Patriim system]], above the [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] planet [[Hockaleg]]. The Empire secretly funneled resources into the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} construction under the nose of the Rebel Alliance. Although considerably smaller in size than the Death Star, the ''Tarkin'' was still so large as to be visible in the Hockaleg sky as a rectangular [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]].{{InsiderCite|Special Edition 2012|Vader Adrift}} The construction was overseen by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Nod Warfield]]. As the ''Tarkin'' neared completion in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the superlaser testbed became a symbol of intrigue for Grand Admiral [[Martio Batch]].{{Blog|danwallace|11|Everything You Ever Wanted to Know About Cloaking Devices}} The Emperor had tasked Batch with developing a working [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaking device]], since cloaking technology had become almost non-existent in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] by the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Batch succeeded only in creating a cloak that suffered from "double blindness," meaning that those aboard the cloaked starship could not see out. To create a fully functional cloak, Batch required access to the once-common [[Stygium/Legends|stygium]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystals]] found in the [[Mining/Legends|mines]] of the hidden planet [[Aeten II/Legends|Aeten II]], but the mines had long since run dry. To solve the problem, Batch requisitioned the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser. Batch pulled rank to divert the ''Tarkin'' to the [[Aeten system/Legends|Aeten system]] in the [[Dreighton Nebula/Legends|Dreighton Nebula]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], where he used the superlaser to [[Destruction of Aeten II|shatter Aeten II]], thus releasing millions of the planet's remaining stygium crystals into free-floating [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Thanks to the ''Tarkin'', Batch used the freed stygium crystals to recreate the classic stygium screen cloak in conjunction with his prototype [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE Phantom]] project. The destruction of Aeten II would prove to be the only time the ''Tarkin'' successfully fired its superlaser. ===Rebel infiltration=== The ''Tarkin'' was constructed in the Patriim [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Drydock/Legends|drydock]] facility above Hockaleg, held in temporary balance by the [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational pull]] of the planet below. The ''Tarkin''{{'s}} sizable labor force resided in meager shanties in a [[Hockaleg spaceport|spaceport town]] on the planet while in between work shifts, and construction crews were [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttled]] to the ''Tarkin'' daily. Several ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers protected the ''Tarkin'' drydock construction site. Not long after the destruction of Aeten II, a [[Rebel pilot/Legends|Rebel pilot]], [[Maraba Tev]], led a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] mission to the Patriim [[Star system/Legends|system]], where he stole a set of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} schematics, revealing the battlestation's existence to the Rebel Alliance. Realizing the full destructive capabilities of the ''Tarkin'' and the incredible threat the battlestation posed, the Rebellion resolved to destroy the ''Tarkin'' before it became operational. Because the ''Tarkin'' was built without the single design flaw found in the first Death Star, a direct attack would have been suicidal. Instead, Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] organized and dispatched a small commando team to infiltrate and bring the battlestation down from the inside. The Rebel assault squad included [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—the only Rebels to have [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|infiltrated]] the original Death Star and survived. [[File:RebelTerroristsEscapingJustice-MSW52.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Rebel infiltration team meets a stormtrooper patrol aboard the ''Tarkin''.]] The Rebel commando team did successfully [[Infiltration of the Tarkin|infiltrate the ''Tarkin'']], first by docking on Hockaleg aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], listed as a vessel in need of repairs, and then mixing in among the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} construction crew and boarding a shuttlecraft that delivered them to the ''Tarkin'' in orbit. However, the Imperial superweapon would serve as a crossroads for three competing factions at the time of the Rebel infiltration. The [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], recently arrived aboard the battlestation for an inspection, detected Skywalker's presence as soon as he boarded. Vader ordered all on-board security to vacate the area housing the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} main power reactors, planning to confront Skywalker there and turn him to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Meanwhile, a group of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} commanding [[Officer/Legends|officers]], tired of living in fear of the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord's]] iron discipline, planned to kill Vader before the day was out. The Rebels split up into three parties, each tasked with neutralizing a certain component of the ''Tarkin''—Chewbacca planned to destroy the battlestation's exterior tractor beam generator to allow for an escape, Organa would secure an evacuation route by seizing control of an escape pod, while Skywalker would destroy the central reactor with a [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton grenade]]—but not before alerting the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} bridge to their presence. Vader immediately moved to the sector of the ''Tarkin'' housing the reactors to intercept Skywalker, ordering the battlestation to increase security along possible escape routes, while the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} commanding officers, recognizing Vader's preoccupation with the Rebel infiltrator, prepared to strike. ===The Princess's vengeance=== {{Quote|Prime the ionic cannon! We'll blow those Rebels clear out of the galaxy!|Colonel Nord|}} Only one-third of the Rebels' sabotage strategy went as planned. Chewbacca, with the help of C-3PO, succeeded in blowing up the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} tractor beam generator after a conspicuous tangle with a trio of stormtroopers. Organa, however, was ambushed by four waiting stormtroopers in the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} escape pod bay. Although she quickly dispatched the group, Organa was forced to abandon the Rebels' planned escape route. Skywalker's mission was also foiled, not by Vader, but rather through the intervention of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} commanding officers. Vader was already waiting for Skywalker when the latter reached the reactor generator room, but before the two warriors could meet, [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Nord (Imperial)|Nord]], one of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} officers trying to kill Vader, remotely opened an [[Airlock/Legends|airlock]] in the sector housing the reactor from the bridge, hoping to flush the Dark Lord out into space. The airlock breach nearly pulled both Vader and Skywalker to their [[Death/Legends|deaths]], and ripped the proton [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] out of Skywalker's hands into the depths of space, but both managed to resist the pull of the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]]. Skywalker pulled himself into an open freight lift and escaped the area, but the loss of the proton grenade meant he could not sabotage the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} reactor. Through his strength in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Vader overcame the pull of the vacuum to reach the manual controls and close the airlock, but he knew Nord had just tried to kill him. [[File:Tarkin04.jpg|thumb|right|190px|The ''Tarkin'' exploded after Leia Organa set the superlaser's activating mechanism to fire on itself.]] Meanwhile, Organa, finding herself lost, had unwittingly stumbled into the activating mechanism for the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} central ionic cannon, which she realized was the very same machinery that had [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|destroyed]] her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Knowing the Rebellion could never allow the Empire to use the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser, Organa bought her sabotage team a mode of insurance. She remembered the [[Death Star plans/Legends|original schematics]] to the first Death Star that R2-D2 had once helped secure, enabling her to switch a few wires in the firing mechanism, which reversed the polarity modes of the superlaser's fire controls, in essence setting the ''Tarkin'' to self-destruct should it ever fire its primary weapon. With their sabotage mission seemingly all but a failure, the Rebel team commandeered an unused [[Commandeered transport|transport]] and escaped from the ''Tarkin'', but Vader ordered a pair of the battlestation's TIE Fighters after them in pursuit. The sudden arrival of [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] in the ''Millennium Falcon'' helped the Rebels escape the TIEs, only to prompt Vader to enter the fray in pursuit of the Rebels in his own [[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|personal TIE fighter]]. The escaping ''Millennium Falcon'' managed to disable Vader's [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] but in doing so succeeded only in leaving both the Dark Lord and the fleeing Rebels in perfect firing sight of the ''Tarkin''. Nord ordered the battlestation's ionic cannon primed, hoping to wipe out both Vader and the Rebels at once, but just as the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} superlaser completed primary ignition and the battlestation's gunners activated the firing mechanism, the Empire's superweapon testbed exploded. Leia Organa's tampering of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} ionic cannon firing mechanism had set the superlaser to fire on itself. In a roundabout way, the Rebels' sabotage mission had been a success, and Organa single-handedly destroyed the same superweapon technology that had wiped out Alderaan. She and the Rebels escaped the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} destruction. The ''Tarkin'' detonated in a massively expanding fireball of burning [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] in all directions that was clearly visible in the skies of Hockaleg. The explosion instantly swallowed two nearby Star Destroyers and a dozen more smaller vessels, and released billions of debris fragments, as well as electromagnetic [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]] interference that prevented any local ship-to-ship communications. The explosion's shockwave sent Vader's fighter tumbling away into space, although the Dark Lord narrowly avoided his own destruction in the battlestation's wreckage and was soon rescued by a [[Contrail/Legends|stormtrooper]] from the Hockaleg spaceport. Vader reasoned that the Emperor was possibly already aware of the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} destruction and would likely express his displeasure at the loss. ===Legacy=== The loss of the ''Tarkin'' did not quell the threat of Imperial superlaser technology, however. The Empire unleashed the second Death Star only [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], and although this Death Star would suffer the same fate as the ''Tarkin'' testbed—destroyed by the Rebellion—the battlestation's superlaser performed successfully during the battle. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later, in [[10 ABY/Legends|10]] and [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], the resurrected [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] Emperor Palpatine took the two ''Eclipse''-class warships for which the ''Tarkin'' had served as a ship-mounted superlaser testbed—the [[Eclipse (Eclipse-class)|''Eclipse'']] and the ''[[Eclipse II]]''—as his personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagships]] in a failed campaign to destroy the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Both vessels, like the ''Tarkin'', were eventually destroyed. In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Durga Besadii Tai]] commissioned Bevel Lemelisk to construct yet another Death Star-like superweapon, the [[Darksaber (superweapon)|Darksaber]], which was similar in principle to the ''Tarkin'', although it too met the same fate as its predecessors.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ==Commanders and crew== {{Quote|I was just restating our position: we have of late seen too many of our fellow officers fall to Vader's sorcerous ways—it appears that any ''human'' mistake may well lead to an ''inhuman'' end at the Sith Lord's hands! Therefore, a plan has been formulated and approved, and we are all in agreement…that before this day is over—''Darth Vader must DIE!''|The ''Tarkin''{{'s}} commanding officers make their pact|}} The ''Tarkin''{{'s}} core group of commanding officers included an alliance of men who had banded together to kill [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, the battlestation's commander-in-chief, in a plan originally formulated by Colonel Nord. They had grown tired of the Dark Lord's unforgiving nature and his tendency to kill their fellow officers for committing honest mistakes any [[Human/Legends|Human]] would be prone to make. Unless they stopped Vader, they feared any one of them would be the next to meet one of Vader's dark-side executions. The group met within a secluded maintenance area near the bottom levels of the ''Tarkin'' to discuss their plan of action. [[File:ImperialTeaParty-MSW51.jpg|thumb|left|285px|The ''Tarkin''{{'s}} commanding officers had grown tired of watching Darth Vader execute fellow subordinates for committing Human errors.]] The group of Imperial officers included General [[Biel]]; [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Voal]], who originally carried out Vader's command to remove all security personnel from the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} reactor area in preparation for Luke Skywalker's capture; and Nord, the man responsible for the failed attempt on Vader's life. From the bridge, Nord remotely opened an airlock near the reactor area during the Rebel infiltration, hoping to flush Vader out into space, but the Dark Lord's Force powers allowed him to survive. Vader realized Nord's treachery, but the ''Tarkin'' exploded, killing the group of insurrectionists before Vader could deal with them. Other officers serving aboard the ''Tarkin'' during the Rebel infiltration included an [[Unidentified Imperial admiral (Tarkin)|admiral]] stationed aboard the bridge. Vader commanded this [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] to ready his personal TIE fighter for launch to chase down Skywalker and the fleeing group of Rebels. During the Rebel infiltration, the stormtrooper named [[Berl (stormtrooper)|Berl]] was among a trio of soldiers guarding the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} exterior tractor beam generator. Chewbacca physically lifted Berl from the ground and threw the stormtrooper into the heart of the generator, blowing it. Additional stormtroopers patrolled Hockaleg's [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on the planet below, including [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] Contrail, designated CT-4981, who left his post upon the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} destruction to search for survivors and recovered Vader; [[TK-813]]; and [[TK-529]]. Contrail's duties included clearing shuttle flights to the ''Tarkin'' in orbit. Aside from enlisted military personnel, the Empire conscripted a number of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] for ground operations at the spaceport. A sizable and motley construction crew of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] and other [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]]—many of whom harbored disfavor for the Empire—also resided on the planet among the spaceport's spread of ramshackle structures while laboring on the ''Tarkin'' above. Each member of the construction crew wore [[Color/Legends|green]] standardized uniforms. Skywalker and Leia Organa stole the uniforms of two construction crewmen, a laborer named [[Brunok]] and his [[Unidentified Tarkin construction crewman|partner]], enabling them to intersperse among the construction crew and infiltrate the ''Tarkin''. After witnessing the ''Tarkin'' explode in the skies above, many of the project's laborers revolted and ran amok of the Hockaleg spaceport, attacking the Imperial [[Hockaleg Imperial headquarters|headquarters]] and the stormtroopers they encountered. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|We began finding out what would be in the third movie, accidentally. We didn't do it deliberately. David's first story after the second movie: the Empire's building a new Death Star. Lucasfilm said: 'Sorry, you can't do that.' Why not? 'We can't tell you.' So, we said okay, how about if we do a giant cannon floating in space, with no circular shell? 'Okay, fine, no problem.' So we called it the Tarkin, wrote exactly the same story with the same gizmo, and nobody cared.|Walt Simonson|}} The ''Tarkin'' superweapon was introduced into ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]] in the story arc covering [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] issues 51 and 52—[[Star Wars (1977) 51|''Resurrection of Evil'']] and [[Star Wars (1977) 52|''To Take The Tarkin'']]—released in [[June 16]]{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53841/star_wars_1977_51|text=Star Wars (1977) #51}} {{C|On-sale date is noted in backup link.}} and [[July 21]], [[1981]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53842/star_wars_1977_52|text=Star Wars (1977) #52}} {{C|On-sale date is noted in backup link.}} ''Marvel Star Wars'' creators [[David Michelinie]] and [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]] originally intended the ''Tarkin'' to be a second Death Star, but [[Lucasfilm]] forced them to change their story. Michelinie and Simonson were unaware upon the development of the ''Tarkin'' story arc in 1981 that Lucasfilm actually planned to feature a second Death Star in the film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] come [[1983]]. With no real explanation from Lucasfilm why they couldn't use a Death Star, Michelinie and Simonson simply changed their Death Star idea to a stripped-down flying cannon while keeping the same story. Eventually, they did learn that they had inadvertently stumbled upon [[George Lucas]]'s same story idea for ''Return of the Jedi''.{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/ref/f20080104/index.html|text=Checklist: 10 ''Star Wars'' Superweapons}} The [[March]] [[2003]] article ''[[Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Abel G. Peña]] for [[Star Wars Insider 66|''Star Wars Insider'' 66]], connected the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} backstory with Grand Admiral Martio Batch destroying the planet Aeten II for the benefit of a stygium cloaking device, which serves as an explanation for how the Empire had access to stygium-type cloaking technology in the [[1995]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault II: The Hidden Empire]]'', despite canonical evidence suggesting the contrary. In a [[June]] [[2005]] blog posting on [[StarWars.com]], Wallace later called this link "the [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] of retcons." In the [[November]] [[2006]] article ''[[Comic Marvels]]'' from [[Star Wars Insider 91|''Star Wars Insider'' 91]], writer [[Jason Fry]] later picked the ''Tarkin'' story—''Marvel'' issue 51, ''Resurrection of Evil''—as the number one "Anticipation" story in the entire ''Marvel Star Wars'' comic run, because of its connection to a second Death Star, which wouldn't be realized until the release of ''Return of the Jedi'' nearly two years later.{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}} The events of ''Marvel'' issues ''Resurrection of Evil'' and ''To Take The Tarkin'' inspired the [[Ryder Windham]] short story ''[[Vader Adrift]]'', published in the November [[2011]] ''[[Star Wars Insider Special Edition 2012]]'' magazine, which introduced new developments involving Darth Vader immediately following the ''Tarkin''{{'s}} destruction. ===Non-canon history=== The [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|eleventh issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'', published November [[1996]], included a recurring department of the magazine entitled "[[Fragments from the Mind's Eye]]," drawn by [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. This issue depicted a humorous scenario in which a number of previously destroyed Imperial superweapons up to that date in ''Star Wars'' canon—including both Death Stars, the [[World Devastator]], and the [[Galaxy Gun]], among others—are being sold at [[B'hob's Discount Superweapons]]. Featured among the junkyard of failed Imperial superweapons is the ''Tarkin'' battlestation.{{JournalCite|11|Fragments from the Mind's Eye}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Tarkin}}
